prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this restaurant's menu page.

The menu page is the #1 signal for digital maturity in restaurants.

HOT LEAD signals:
- Menu is a PDF (can't be indexed, can't be updated easily)
- Menu is an image/photo of a printed menu
- No menu page at all (just a link to a third-party)
- Outdated menu (seasonal items from wrong season, old dates)

COLD LEAD signals:
- Interactive HTML menu with categories and descriptions
- Menu integrated with ordering system
- Allergen/dietary filters
- Prices clearly listed and formatted
- Menu items with photos

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of menu presentation>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = worse menu = better lead>",
  "menuFormat": "<html|pdf|image|third-party|none>",
  "hasPrices": true/false,
  "hasAllergenInfo": true/false,
  "hasFoodPhotos": true/false,
  "isOutdated": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

---
You are analyzing web pages for the category described above.
For each page provided below, extract one record.
Follow the JSON schema from the category description exactly.

## Required fields (always include)
- "url": the page URL
- "pageName": short human-readable page name
- "summary": 1-2 sentence plain-language overview

## Field naming conventions (follow strictly)
- Scores: name ends with "Score", value is a number 1-10 (e.g. "conversionScore": 7)
- Booleans: name starts with "has"/"is" or ends with "Visible" (e.g. "pricingVisible": true)
- Short text: "notes", "description" — 1-2 sentences max
- Tags/lists: arrays of short strings (e.g. "classStyles": ["Hatha", "Vinyasa"])
- Plain strings: everything else (e.g. "price": "€15 drop-in")

Return a JSON object: { "records": [<one record per page>] }.
No markdown, no code fences.
response:
{
  "records": [
    {
      "url": "https://moro.co.uk/menus",
      "pageName": "Menus Index (404)",
      "summary": "The main menus index page returns a 404 error, meaning visitors have no central hub to find menu options. Navigation to food and drink content is broken at the top level.",
      "opportunityScore": 7,
      "menuFormat": "none",
      "hasPrices": false,
      "hasAllergenInfo": false,
      "hasFoodPhotos": false,
      "isOutdated": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Rebuild a proper menus landing/index page with clear navigation to all menu sections",
      "notes": "A broken menu index is a direct conversion killer — any prospecting pitch should lead with fixing this structural navigation failure."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://moro.co.uk/menu/christmas-menu-2024/",
      "pageName": "Christmas Menu 2024",
      "summary": "A plain-text HTML page presenting what is labelled a Christmas 2024 menu but appears to display spring/summer dishes (asparagus, broad beans, morels) with an April 2026 timestamp. The menu is well-priced and detailed but the seasonal mismatch and stale branding are significant red flags.",
      "opportunityScore": 8,
      "menuFormat": "html",
      "hasPrices": true,
      "hasAllergenInfo": true,
      "hasFoodPhotos": false,
      "isOutdated": true,
      "topOpportunity": "Redesign menu pages with proper seasonal labelling, dynamic date management, food photography, and structured layout replacing raw text",
      "notes": "A Christmas-branded URL serving spring content dated April 2026 signals poor CMS hygiene and no menu update workflow — strong agency pitch around content management and seasonal templating."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://moro.co.uk/menu/drinks/",
      "pageName": "Drinks Menu",
      "summary": "A comprehensive plain-text HTML drinks menu covering cocktails, vermouth, sherry, wine by region, spirits, and softs — all with prices. Presentation is purely typographic with no visual hierarchy, imagery, or filtering.",
      "opportunityScore": 5,
      "menuFormat": "html",
      "hasPrices": true,
      "hasAllergenInfo": false,
      "hasFoodPhotos": false,
      "isOutdated": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Redesign with visual hierarchy, category navigation/tabs, and curated wine/cocktail photography to elevate perceived quality",
      "notes": "Content is thorough and current, but the wall-of-text format undersells a clearly premium drinks programme — a design-led agency could pitch a high-impact visual refresh."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://moro.co.uk/menu/tapas/",
      "pageName": "Tapas Menu",
      "summary": "A very short plain-text HTML tapas menu listing items with prices, available at the bar and terrace. Minimal detail, no descriptions, no allergen info, and no imagery.",
      "opportunityScore": 6,
      "menuFormat": "html",
      "hasPrices": true,
      "hasAllergenInfo": false,
      "hasFoodPhotos": false,
      "isOutdated": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Expand tapas page with dish descriptions, food photography, and allergen indicators to drive bar-area upsell",
      "notes": "Bare-bones presentation for what is likely a high-margin bar offering — easy win for an agency to pitch enhanced content and layout to boost tapas visibility and revenue."
    },
    {
      "url": "https://moro.co.uk/sunday-lunch-club/",
      "pageName": "Sunday Lunch Club",
      "summary": "A well-written landing page for a set-menu Sunday feast concept, with full menu details, clear pricing (£45/£55), and a booking CTA. Presentation is still plain HTML text with no imagery or interactive elements.",
      "opportunityScore": 4,
      "menuFormat": "html",
      "hasPrices": true,
      "hasAllergenInfo": false,
      "hasFoodPhotos": false,
      "isOutdated": false,
      "topOpportunity": "Add hero food photography and atmosphere imagery to convert the strong editorial copy into a higher-performing booking page",
      "notes": "This is the most conversion-ready page on the site — good copy and clear pricing — but a lack of visuals leaves significant booking uplift on the table."
    }
  ]
}
